description:
Canonical end-to-end system correctness dashboard for Chrono Queue. Proves that every job produced is fully accounted for: either successfully consumed by job-worker or persisted to the DLQ after exhausting retries. The fundamental invariant is: Produced = Consumed(success) + DLQ(persisted). Any deviation signals job loss, pipeline stall, or counter drift.

How to use: Check the Imbalance Threshold Alert (top row) first — green means balanced within tolerance. Drill into the Balance Range Totals to see absolute counts, then the Pipeline Delivery Status section to verify Kafka lag is draining. The Processing Activity section shows processing vs failure vs retry rates over time. Use the Loki cross-check row to validate Prometheus counters against log-line counts independently. Adjust the Imbalance Threshold variable to tune alert sensitivity for your SLO.

panels.description: Total jobs sent to Kafka by job-producer in the selected time range. Includes retry re-publishes from retry-dispatcher if any.
panels.title: Jobs Produced

panels.description: Composite correctness score (0-100): penalties for imbalance, lag, and retry failures.
panels.title: Pipeline Health Score

targets.expr: clamp_max(clamp_min(100 - (2 * clamp_min((sum(increase(chrono_jobs_produced_total{service=~"${producer_service:regex}"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) - (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_consumed_total{service=~"${worker_service:regex}", result="success"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) - (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_dlq_total{service=~"${dlq_service:regex}"}[$__range])) or vector(0)), 0)) - (0.05 * (max_over_time((sum(kafka_consumer_fetch_manager_records_lag_max{service=~"${worker_service:regex}|${dlq_service:regex}"}) or vector(0))[$__range:]))) - (30 * (1 - ((sum(increase(retry_producer_requeue_success_total{service=~"${retry_job:regex}"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) / clamp_min((sum(increase(retry_producer_requeue_success_total{service=~"${retry_job:regex}"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) + (sum(increase(retry_producer_requeue_failure_total{service=~"${retry_job:regex}"}[$__range])) or vector(0)), 1e-9)))) , 0), 100)

panels.description: Percentage of produced jobs that are not yet accounted for by successful consumption or DLQ persistence over the selected time range.
panels.title: Unaccounted Jobs %

targets.expr: 100 * (((sum(increase(chrono_jobs_produced_total{service=~"${producer_service:regex}"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) - (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_consumed_total{service=~"${worker_service:regex}", result="success"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) - (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_dlq_total{service=~"${dlq_service:regex}"}[$__range])) or vector(0))) / clamp_min((sum(increase(chrono_jobs_produced_total{service=~"${producer_service:regex}"}[$__range])) or vector(0)), 1))

panels.description: Jobs successfully processed by job-worker in the selected time range.
panels.title: Jobs Consumed (Success)

panels.description: Jobs that exhausted all retries and were persisted to the DLQ in the selected time range.
panels.title: Jobs in DLQ

panels.description: Critical correctness invariant over selected time range. Exactly 0 means all produced jobs are accounted for by success or DLQ.
panels.title: Balance = Produced - (Success + DLQ)

targets.expr: round((sum(increase(chrono_jobs_produced_total{service=~"${producer_service:regex}"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) - (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_consumed_total{service=~"${worker_service:regex}", result="success"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) - (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_dlq_total{service=~"${dlq_service:regex}"}[$__range])) or vector(0)))

panels.description: Hard alert visualization: positive value means unaccounted jobs exceed configured threshold over selected window.
panels.title: Imbalance > threshold

targets.expr: clamp_min(round((sum(increase(chrono_jobs_produced_total{service=~"${producer_service:regex}"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) - (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_consumed_total{service=~"${worker_service:regex}", result="success"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) - (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_dlq_total{service=~"${dlq_service:regex}"}[$__range])) or vector(0))) - ${imbalance_threshold}, 0)

panels.description: Percentage of produced jobs that were fully resolved — either processed successfully by job-worker or persisted to the DLQ after exhausting retries. Should be 100% within any complete time window. A value below 99% means jobs are still in-flight, stuck in consumer lag, or were lost. SLO target: 100% resolution rate.
panels.title: Job Resolution Rate (SLO)

targets.expr: 100 * ((sum(increase(chrono_jobs_consumed_total{service=~"${worker_service:regex}", result="success"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) + (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_dlq_total{service=~"${dlq_service:regex}"}[$__range])) or vector(0))) / clamp_min((sum(increase(chrono_jobs_produced_total{service=~"${producer_service:regex}"}[$__range])) or vector(0)), 1)

panels.description: Real-time correctness trend using consistent rate interval for produced, consumed success, DLQ, and imbalance.

targets.expr: (sum(rate(chrono_jobs_produced_total{service=~"${producer_service:regex}"}[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)) - (sum(rate(chrono_jobs_consumed_total{service=~"${worker_service:regex}", result="success"}[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)) - (sum(rate(chrono_jobs_dlq_total{service=~"${dlq_service:regex}"}[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0))

panels.title: Produced vs Consumed vs DLQ — Count Trend
Pipeline Delivery Status — Are All Jobs Consumed?

panels.description: Consumer lag across all job topics for job-worker. A lag of 0 means all produced Kafka records have been picked up (though not necessarily committed to success/DLQ yet). Non-zero lag means records are still queued.
panels.title: Worker — Max Consumer Lag

panels.description: Consumer lag for the DLQ service. A lag of 0 means all failed jobs pushed to the DLQ topic have been consumed and persisted to MongoDB.
panels.title: DLQ Service — Max Consumer Lag

panels.description: All Kafka records are fully processed when both worker lag AND DLQ lag are 0 simultaneously.
panels.title: Total Lag (Worker + DLQ)

panels.description: Pipeline flow rates in real-time. Produced should be covered by consumed success plus DLQ over time.
panels.title: Produced vs Consumed vs DLQ (stacked)

panels.description: Consumer lag over time per job topic for job-worker. Should converge to 0 once production stops.
panels.title: Consumer Lag by Topic — job-worker

panels.description: Breakdown of produced, consumed, and DLQ'd jobs per job type within the selected range.
panels.title: Produced vs Consumed vs DLQ by Job Type (Range)

panels.description: Log-line counts from Loki to cross-verify the Prometheus counters. All three totals should satisfy: produced ≈ success + DLQ.
panels.title: Log-Based Job Counts (cross-check with Prometheus counters above)
job-worker — Jobs Processed vs Failed
panels.description: Log-based rate of jobs processed, failed, and scheduled for retry by job-worker.

panels.title: Retry Rate and Retry Success %
panels.description: Real-time retry behavior from retry-dispatcher counters: attempt rate and success ratio.

panels.title: Kafka Lag vs Imbalance Correlation
panels.description: Correlates total pipeline lag with real-time imbalance rate. Rising lag with positive imbalance indicates delivery backlog risk.

targets.expr: clamp_min((sum(rate(chrono_jobs_produced_total{service=~"${producer_service:regex}"}[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)) - (sum(rate(chrono_jobs_consumed_total{service=~"${worker_service:regex}", result="success"}[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)) - (sum(rate(chrono_jobs_dlq_total{service=~"${dlq_service:regex}"}[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)), 0)

list.description: Minimum absolute job delta that triggers the Imbalance Threshold Alert panel. Lower values catch small losses early; higher values reduce noise during normal in-flight lag.
list.label: Imbalance Threshold (jobs)
list.name: imbalance_threshold

title: Chrono Queue — End-to-End System Correctness
